title: Limonium durieui
intro: Limonium durieui, also known as Sea Lavender or Statice, is a perennial plant belonging to the Plumbaginaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and ability to thrive in saline environments. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the management of respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Limonium durieui include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to support skin health and wound care.
taxonomy: Limonium durieui is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. This plant is native to the Mediterranean region and is commonly found in coastal areas. It is a shrub-like plant that produces small, white or pink flowers. Limonium durieui is often used as an ornamental plant in gardens due to its attractive flowers and ability to thrive in saline environments. It is also known by its synonym Statice durieui.
